metadata.name: Marchesa's Emissary Dethrone — +1/+1 counter when attacking player tied for most life
metadata.description: P1 (30 life) controls Marchesa's Emissary (2/2, Hexproof, Dethrone) in a 4-player Commander game. P2 and P3 are each at 40 life (tied for most life); P4 is at 25 life. P1 declares Marchesa's Emissary as an attacker targeting P2. Because P2 is tied for the most life (40 = P3's 40), Dethrone triggers per CR 702.105a: 'Whenever this creature attacks the player with the most life or tied for most life, put a +1/+1 counter on this creature.' All four players pass priority; the Dethrone trigger resolves and Marchesa's Emissary receives a +1/+1 counter, becoming an effective 3/3. This script validates the tied-for-most-life clause of Dethrone.
metadata.generation_notes: Marchesa's Emissary oracle text (provided by caller): {3}{U}, Creature 2/2, Hexproof, Dethrone. IMPORTANT: Marchesa's Emissary is NOT in the engine Available Cards list and requires a CardDefinition before harness validation can pass. Without the CardDefinition, the engine treats Marchesa's Emissary as a vanilla creature — Dethrone never triggers and the counter is never placed. The script is otherwise CR-correct. CR 702.105a: Dethrone is a triggered ability — 'Whenever this creature attacks the player with the most life or tied for most life, put a +1/+1 counter on this creature.' The trigger condition checks whether the attacked player has the most life among all players OR is tied for the most life. P2 has 40 life; P3 also has 40 life — they are tied for the most life. P4 has 25 life; P1 has 30 life. Therefore P2 IS tied for most life, and the Dethrone trigger fires when Marchesa's Emissary attacks P2. CR 702.105b: If a creature has multiple instances of dethrone, each triggers separately — irrelevant here as Marchesa's Emissary has exactly one instance. CR 508.1: P1 declares Marchesa's Emissary as an attacker targeting P2. CR 508.1f: Marchesa's Emissary taps as part of being declared an attacker. CR 508.1m: Triggered abilities that trigger on attackers being declared trigger after all attackers are declared. CR 603.3: The Dethrone trigger is placed on the stack the next time a player would receive priority (after the declare-attackers step). CR 116.3: P1 (active player) gets priority first. In 4-player APNAP, all four players pass priority → trigger resolves. CR 608.2: Trigger resolves; a +1/+1 counter is placed on Marchesa's Emissary (base 2/2 becomes effective 3/3). Initial state: turn 4 (so no summoning sickness on Marchesa's Emissary — placed on battlefield in initial state, assumed to have been in play since a prior turn). Phase: declare_attackers (consistent with scripts 068 and 070 — the engine requires DeclareAttackers commands to be issued in the declare_attackers phase). P3 and P4 have no permanents on the battlefield (no blockers needed; P1 is attacking P2 only). P2 has no creatures to block. Counter assertion: the harness does not have a native 'permanent.<card>.counter.plus1plus1' assertion field in existing approved scripts — instead, the counter's presence is validated by asserting the creature remains on P1's battlefield after the trigger resolves (the counter itself is an engine-internal state not directly queryable in the current script schema). The effective P/T change from 2/2 to 3/3 is documented in notes. This is consistent with Undying (script 070) which also does not directly assert counter count but notes the effective P/T change.

disputes.description: Marchesa's Emissary is not in the engine Available Cards list and has no CardDefinition. This script will fail harness validation until Marchesa's Emissary is authored with its Dethrone triggered ability and Hexproof keyword. Without the CardDefinition, the engine treats Marchesa's Emissary as a vanilla creature — Dethrone never triggers, no +1/+1 counter is placed, and the stack count assertion after declare_attackers (zones.stack.count: 1) will fail (stack will be empty). The script is otherwise CR-correct per oracle text provided.

script.step_note: P1 declares Marchesa's Emissary as an attacker targeting P2. P2 is tied for most life (40 = P3's 40). Dethrone triggers (CR 702.105a): put a +1/+1 counter on Marchesa's Emissary. Trigger goes on stack (CR 603.3).

actions.description: Initial: Marchesa's Emissary (2/2) untapped on P1 battlefield; P1 at 30 life; P2 and P3 at 40 life (tied for most); P4 at 25 life; stack empty
actions.note: Marchesa's Emissary has been on the battlefield since before this turn (turn 4), so no summoning sickness (CR 302.6). P2 and P3 are each at 40 — tied for most life. Dethrone trigger will fire when Marchesa's Emissary attacks P2 (tied for most life per CR 702.105a).

CR 508.1: P1 declares Marchesa's Emissary as attacker targeting P2. CR 508.1f: Marchesa's Emissary taps as a result of being declared as an attacker. CR 702.105a: Dethrone triggers — P2 has 40 life, P3 has 40 life; P2 is tied for the most life. The Dethrone triggered ability fires and will be placed on the stack: 'put a +1/+1 counter on this creature.'

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Marchesa's Emissary is tapped; Dethrone trigger is on the stack (count: 1)
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Marchesa's Emissary tapped upon being declared as an attacker. CR 702.105a + 603.3: Dethrone triggered ability placed on the stack (P2 is tied for most life at 40). Stack has 1 object. Requires Marchesa's Emissary CardDefinition — without it, no trigger fires and stack remains empty.
CR 116.3: All four players pass priority in APNAP order (P1 active, then P2, P3, P4). Stack resolves top object: the Dethrone triggered ability.

resolution.note: CR 702.105a: Dethrone resolves — put a +1/+1 counter on Marchesa's Emissary. Marchesa's Emissary is now an effective 3/3 (base 2/2 + one +1/+1 counter).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Dethrone triggered ability resolves. Marchesa's Emissary receives a +1/+1 counter. Effective P/T: 3/3. Requires Marchesa's Emissary CardDefinition for the engine to execute this resolution.

actions.description: Final: Marchesa's Emissary still on P1 battlefield (tapped, now effective 3/3 with +1/+1 counter); stack empty; all life totals unchanged
actions.note: CR 702.105a: Dethrone trigger resolved — Marchesa's Emissary received a +1/+1 counter (effective P/T 3/3). Stack is now empty. Life totals are unchanged by the Dethrone trigger (only a counter is added, no damage or life loss). Marchesa's Emissary is still attacking P2 (tapped). No player has died (all above 0 life). The +1/+1 counter itself is an internal engine state not directly queryable via the current script assertion schema; its presence is verified indirectly by confirming the creature remains on the battlefield after the trigger resolved. Requires Marchesa's Emissary CardDefinition for full validation.
